name: Can I convert a tub to a walk‑in shower in a small Peninsula bathroom without hurting resale?

mainEntity.name: In a small bathroom in Pacifica or Daly City, is it a good idea to remove the bathtub and install a walk‑in shower only, and in what situations could this hurt resale value?
mainEntity.text: In a small bathroom in Pacifica or Daly City, is it a good idea to remove the bathtub and install a walk‑in shower only, and in what situations could this hurt resale value?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tub‑to‑shower conversions are popular in small Pacifica and Daly City bathrooms because walk‑in showers feel more open and are easier to access. However, the impact on resale depends on your overall home and target buyer. Removing a tub in a home that still has another full bathroom with a tub is usually acceptable and may even be a selling point for adult or downsizing buyers. If your home has only one bathroom or one tub, eliminating it can reduce appeal to families with small children who rely on baths. In areas where many buyers are young families, this could narrow your buyer pool and affect resale value. Before deciding, inventory all bathrooms in the house. If you will still have a tub elsewhere, focus on designing a high‑quality, low‑threshold shower with proper waterproofing, non‑slip tile, and a frameless glass panel to make your small bathroom feel bigger. If this is your only chance for a tub, weigh how long you plan to stay, your own needs, and who is most likely to buy the home next. Discuss local buyer expectations with a real‑estate professional and share that guidance with your contractor so the remodel supports both your lifestyle and future resale.
acceptedAnswer.description: Converting a tub to a walk‑in shower in a small Peninsula bathroom works best when the home still has at least one tub elsewhere and the target buyers are adults. Removing the only tub can hurt appeal for families with young children. Check your home’s layout, likely buyer, and local norms before eliminating the last bathtub.
